C[Edwina: Awwl, B's S10 looks really bad. Q8 looks so much better.
C[Edwina: yep, that's the correct punishment for B's stupid S10. (All intuitive judgement)
C[Edwina: W should play sabaki (loose shapely play) in strong enemy forces to get out quickly in sente, but B failed to take advantage of W's weak shape in later play.
C[Edwina: Awwwl, what poor timing for B P16!  G17 much better.
C[Edwina: Trying to get both sides alive looks too ambitious.
C[Edwina: I was getting concerned at this point. B has 50 solid points but W has a sente sequence putting pressure on the 2 B stones. I suspect W will be winning.
C[Edwina: N12 looks wrong: it forced W to get rid of an aji without significant advantage. Should have been M17 to take away eye & territory of B, & settle the W group.
Foolish Tesuji gave away your 1st chance of clear victory.  :D
C[Edwina: B did N18 with a sigh of relieve.
C[Edwina: and it is infectious (foolishness that is  :D )
M16 haunted B later in the game. Should have been L16.
C[Edwina: Awwwwwl, B's M11 is aji keshi - elimination of oppenent's weakness!
Edwina: Or may be it wasn't too bad. It strengthen B's group in sente, & get to play an extension in gote.
C[Edwina: I was confident with C10 that the game is now in B's favour - but just marginally.
C[Edwina: O, K9 was so intimidating to a lady!
C[Edwina: W's potential territory is now a major worry for B.
C[Edwina: I feel F11 should have been F12, giving Edwina a 2nd chance to turn the game around.  :D
C[Edwina: J17 is a forced B into a better shape for living, but still left the cutting point G17 open for later punishment.
C[Edwina: W is still winnning! Hack!
C[Edwina: A critical move - O7 to escape, but the group would live without O7. Should have defened at cut G17.
C[Edwina: W resigned, but shouldn't because B will be in gote after W F17 & W F18. I think W should win by 2 about 2 points. Hence Edwina was 3rd time lucky!  :D

C[Isao: Here's where the troubles began. I would play C17, E17 or F15 to simply go to the center.
C[Isao: Now I have a lot of territory on the left, safe group at the top and a (soon to be) dragon to chase :)
C[Isao: First make sure you are safe and then attack. If W can kill G16 group he can sacrifice O17 (which still has R17 aji).
C[Isao: After this you live and have some territory on your own, so I would say you did quite good. White has strong influence though and can be sure he won't die anywhere.
C[Isao: M3 would be better, with this you are attacking, but without any purpose,  you can't kill the M4 stone and you are gaining nothing from N5.
C[Isao: I was pretty sure it can't live, but since I was ahead I didn't want to risk :)
C[Isao: My next attack would be this group, nice timing :) Thanks for the game it was fun :)

C[MatCauthon: This turned into a disaster for White.  Probably better to just play Q14.  You'll have P16 or R11 next to stay out of serious trouble.
C[MatCauthon: I think you want to put some pressure on F3 first - you should be able to do that in sente and then get back to this point.
C[MatCauthon: D5 sure gives you a lot of points on the side.  Maybe play that instead of trying to kill?
C[MatCauthon: Even though you got a second move here, I still lived, so G2 probably wasn't a good idea.
C[MatCauthon: I don't think you can afford this move.  D12 might save the group on the inside.
